rows.notes: Very clear centered year at top of page, no amounts present. Strong legibility and unambiguous.
Clear title just below the year. No monetary columns on this line.
Carried-forward balance clearly written as '690 3 0' at the top of the amounts column. Vertical ruling lines used as alignment reference per page guidance.
Category label at left margin introducing the following group of entries. Legible handwriting; no amounts on this row.

Name 'Robey' legible in central column; amount reads as '1 16 0' aligned under the pounds/shillings/pence ruling (columns referenced to the clear Total line). Minor ambiguity in the surname loop but digits are clear.

Handwriting of name is moderately ambiguous (reads as 'Hodder' or similar). Amount aligned to columns and appears as '1 6 0' but the first digit of pounds is faint; chosen reading based on alignment with ruling lines.
Surname 'Bates' clear. Amount reads '1 4 6' with digits well aligned; shillings and pence are within valid ranges.
Description 'Thatching' clear. Amount appears similar to the previous line '1 4 6' though the pence digit is slightly smudged; chosen based on alignment with vertical ruling lines.

Two-column description ('Kid Cottage' on left, 'Comm:' in center) recorded verbatim. Amount reads '3 6' in the shilling/pence area with no pounds written (interpreted as 0 pounds, 3s, 6d). Slight uncertainty because pounds column is blank but vertical alignment supports this reading.

Description reads as 'Gardner' with note 'Quit Rent ...' (handwriting of place ambiguous). Amount interpreted as '2 1 0' but the place name and the tens place of shillings are slightly unclear; alignment with columns used to place digits.

Central entry 'Inn Bill' paired with surname (reads 'Gwypnear'/'Gwynear' variant). Amount '4 18 10' is legible and within valid currency ranges; slight uncertainty on spelling of name.
Label 'Comm:r' (commissioner/commission) is clear; amount reads '12 18 4' aligned to rulings. All digits valid.
Short description 'Stamps & Poles' clear. Amount small, reads as '1s 6d' with pounds column blank (interpreted as 0); ink is faint in the pounds area but column alignment supports this reading.
Description partly abbreviated; amount reads as '1 6 0' though digits are slightly crowded. Alignment with ruling lines used to place each figure.
Name 'Stone' and 'Wheeler' legible. Amount '1 5 6' reads clearly from columns.
Combined description preserved. Amount '3 13 5' is clear and aligned to the pence column.
Description legible. Amount '4 12 6' appears clearly in the pounds/shillings/pence columns.
Name 'Lovell' and 'Hatching Cottages' readable. Amount '2 2 6' aligns with rule lines and is clear.
This line appears to be a continuation/related sub-item ('Straw for'). Amount reads as '4 0 4' but the pence digit is faint; placed using the strong vertical alignment of the Total row as reference.

Description 'Receiving Cottage Rent' clear. Amount reads '12 6 0' though the pence column is faint; alignment with ruling lines used to place the '0' pence.

A short line immediately following the Receiving Cottage Rent shows '13 0' in the amounts. The left-side description is extremely faint/absent; extracted as an entry with amount only. Possibility this is continuation or a separate small item; recorded due to visible amounts.

Line reads 'By sale at New' (or 'By sale at Newm') with a large figure '8000' in the pounds column. The large number is clearly written and aligns with the pounds ruling.

Text 'Assumed by Domus' readable. Amount '858 12 4' appears and aligns under the pounds/shillings/pence rulings. Clear handwriting for the figures.
Legacy entry for 'E Jones' legible; amount '101 0 0' clear. Abbreviations preserved as written.

Entry referencing 'Mrs Jennison'. The figure appears as '187 16 0' though there is moderate ambiguity in the centering and whether it reads '187' or '187/...' — chosen '187 16 0' based on alignment with ruling lines and plausible arithmetic for a legacy plus interest.

Line shows two figures in prose '3.10.4 - 1.10.0' and a summed amount at right '5 0 4' which is clearly visible; extracted the summed amount. Description copied as written; figures in the text are interpreted as components.

Description 'Walsh Stone pit Kidlington' legible; large amount '70 0 0' clear in pounds column.
Phrase 'grant for schools' clear; amount '5 0 0' reads cleanly and aligns to ruling lines.
Text 'Willan special grant for Do.' clearly written. Amount '2 2 0' visible and unambiguous.

Final total line at the bottom of the page clearly shows '10546 0 1/2'. The ruling lines here are very distinct (page-specific guidance), used as the primary alignment reference. The fractional pence '1/2' is visible and recorded as 0.5.
